component.name: Drawer
component.description: A vaul-powered bottom drawer that slides up from the screen edge with a drag handle and ModalBase glass-top treatment. Use when a mobile interaction requires one focused decision or confirmation.

useCases: Single-choice selection (e.g. pick a payout frequency)
Simple confirmation before a consequential action
One-field quick edit (e.g. rename a record)
Mobile counterpart to a Dialog via ResponsiveDialog with mobileVariant='drawer'

commonPatterns.name: single-choice
commonPatterns.description: A drawer presenting a small list of mutually exclusive options.
commonPatterns.composition:
Select planChoose the plan that fits your team.
{plans.map(p => )}

commonPatterns.name: confirmation
commonPatterns.description: A drawer asking a single yes/no question before proceeding.
commonPatterns.composition: <Drawer><DrawerTrigger asChild><Button variant="outline">Archive</Button></DrawerTrigger><DrawerContent><DrawerHeader><DrawerTitle>Archive this record?</DrawerTitle><DrawerDescription>It will be hidden from your active list but can be restored later.</DrawerDescription></DrawerHeader><DrawerFooter><DrawerClose asChild><Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button></DrawerClose><Button variant="primary">Archive</Button></DrawerFooter></DrawerContent></Drawer>

commonPatterns.name: responsive-dialog
commonPatterns.description: Used as the mobile counterpart to a Dialog via ResponsiveDialog.
commonPatterns.composition:
Payout scheduleChoose how often funds are transferred.
...
CancelSave

antiPatterns.reason: A drawer has limited vertical space and no scroll affordance by default. Three or more distinct fields or decisions overwhelm the surface.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use ResponsiveDialog with mobileVariant='sheet' for flows with 3+ questions or choices — the full-viewport Sheet provides the necessary room.

antiPatterns.reason: Drawers can be dismissed by dragging down, which is too easy for irreversible actions.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use AlertDialog for destructive confirmations that require explicit acknowledgement.

antiPatterns.reason: Drawer is optimised for mobile touch interaction. On desktop the dragging affordance is unnatural.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Dialog on desktop. ResponsiveDialog handles the switching automatically.

antiPatterns.reason: A drawer that navigates away when dismissed breaks expected back-navigation behaviour.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a full-page route or Sheet side='left' for navigation panels.

slots.DrawerTrigger: Element that opens the drawer (use asChild to wrap your own button)
slots.DrawerContent: The sliding panel with ModalBase glass-top treatment and drag handle. Wraps children in an inner bg-card surface.
slots.DrawerHeader: Optional top section; holds DrawerTitle and DrawerDescription
slots.DrawerDescription: Optional muted supporting text below the title
slots.DrawerFooter: Optional bottom section for action buttons
slots.DrawerClose: Wraps elements that should close the drawer on click
slots.DrawerPortal: Portals the drawer to document body (rendered automatically inside DrawerContent)

parentConstraints: Drawer is a root-level overlay; it portals to the document body via DrawerPortal

interactions.open: Triggered by DrawerTrigger click or controlled open prop
interactions.close: Drag the panel downward past the dismiss threshold, click the overlay, or press Escape. DrawerClose elements also dismiss.
interactions.drag: Vaul handles spring-physics dragging. The drag handle pill at the top signals draggability.

responsive.mobile: Full-width bottom drawer; natural content height; glass top edge with ModalBase tokens
responsive.desktop: Not intended for direct desktop use. On desktop, ResponsiveDialog renders a Dialog instead.

accessibility.role: dialog (via vaul, which wraps Radix Dialog)
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Escape closes the drawer; Tab traps focus within the open drawer; Shift+Tab cycles backwards
accessibility.screenReader: DrawerTitle provides the accessible name; DrawerDescription provides the accessible description
accessibility.focusManagement: Focus is trapped inside the drawer while open; returns to trigger element on close

rules: Use Drawer only when the mobile interaction involves one focused decision — a single question, a single-choice list, or a simple confirmation.
If the flow requires 3 or more distinct questions or choices, use ResponsiveDialog with mobileVariant='sheet' instead so the user has the full screen.
Two questions can go either way — use your judgment based on how complex each question is.
Always include DrawerTitle inside DrawerContent for accessibility.
Do not use Drawer for destructive/irreversible confirmations — use AlertDialog.
Do not nest Drawers inside other Drawers or Sheets.
alternatives: AlertDialog — for destructive confirmations requiring explicit acknowledgement
ResponsiveDialog (mobileVariant='sheet') — for flows with 3+ questions or choices on mobile
Dialog — for non-destructive overlays on desktop
Sheet — for slide-in panels from any edge (right, left, top, bottom)

aiHints.context: Reach for Drawer (or ResponsiveDialog mobileVariant='drawer') on mobile when the user must make one focused decision — a confirmation, a single-choice pick, or a one-field edit. The rule of thumb: 1 question → drawer; 3+ questions or choices → full Sheet (mobileVariant='sheet'). Two questions can go either way based on complexity. On desktop, always render a Dialog instead — ResponsiveDialog handles the swap automatically.
